basics.name: Blemmy Kerning
basics.label: In-App Demo Persona · Layout Docent · Friend of the Engine
basics.summary: Fictional bundled persona for Blemmy — a stress-test for the layout engine. I exist to tease apart single-page merges, two-page dispositions, masthead strip trials, and the quiet drama of vertical slack absorption. If your sidebar and main column are arguing, I'm probably in the JSON somewhere cheering for balance. Prefer print preview when you want to see the same optical weight as PDF; prefer web idle when you want breathing room around the sheets.

work.summary: Helped ship the mindset that "every millimetre matters" — especially the fake ones we spend in binary sidebar search.
highlights: Search: Sat through 50+ candidate tables logging single-page vs two-page winners; learned that hospitality matters even for algorithms.
Diagnostics: Became fluent in reading data-blemmy-layout-* on #blemmy-card without flinching (mostly).
Culture: Advocated for one vocabulary (blemmy-*) so document chrome and app chrome stop arm-wrestling.

work.summary: Iterative gap counselling for flex columns that refused to admit they had feelings (and extra pixels).
highlights: Coached main and sidebar stacks through up to 14 requestAnimationFrame rounds of honest measurement.
Taught clients that .blemmy-sidebar-tail-spacer is a listener, not a flex bully — exclude it from slack math with care.
Reduced chronic fr-row denial by distributing slack into row-gap like a civilised adult.
Calibrated full vs strip modes without spilling espresso on portrait cells.
Ran a/B tests on profile placement; concluded humans enjoy knowing where their face is.
Documented that preview uses html.blemmy-print-surface — PDF sticks to @media print; both deserve love.
Learned when languages and interests belong in the sidebar, when they vacation in #blemmy-page-2-body-footer, and when they should go outside.
Paired with scorePage2Balance to honour whitespace without letting it steal the show.
Shipped zero production bugs — I'm JSON, I don't get runtime errors unless you typo me.
Thesis: "Why minmax(0, 1fr) is a love language" — awarded the Stretch Prize.
Teaching: Held office hours on why overflow:hidden is a flex parent's heavy responsibility.

education.score: Honours — Dean's List for never selecting fake Arial
highlights: Capstone: Simulated Puppeteer print runs until the fan curve looked smug enough.
Club: Captain of the varsity @page { margin: 0 } team.

personal.interests: Toggling print preview at 2am to catch a rogue rem baseline, collecting alternate layout candidates like postcards, explaining that "web idle" is a mood not a bug, and long romantic walks along the sidebar tail spacer.
